The Audit Team emphasized that COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 requires the release and utilization of the total CF to be covered by a resolution duly approved by 2/3 of the total membership of the LPOC. Verification of the documents submitted revealed that the submitted minutes of the meeting did not show that 2/3 votes of the LPOC approved the POP and the release of the CF. Since a resolution is required, the Audit Team is of the opinion that the actual members of the LPOC shall constitute a quorum in order to pass the said resolution. Moreover, the Joint Circular required the certified copy of Annual/ Supplemental Budget supported with the AIP, showing the allocation/ budget for POP of the City and the corresponding Appropriation Ordinance approving the budget. Hence, allocation/ budget for the POP shall be identified and specifically stated in the Annual/Supplemental Budget and the AIP.

stated in Section 54.6 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of
R.A. 9184 which is the submission of PhilGEPS Certificate of Registration as a
condition for award of contract.

Management’s Comment(s):
It is only recently that a gasoline station in Bogo City acquired a PhilGEPS registration.
Starting CY 2018, the City’s fuel will be purchased there.

We recommend that Management direct the BAC to adopt public bidding as a
customary mode of procurement and stop the practice of resorting to alternative
mode of procurement unless otherwise in exceptional cases. Likewise adhere to
Sections 10, 48, 53, 9 and 54.6 of the Revised IRR.

Management’s Comment(s):
The BAC Chairperson committed that starting this year, purchases of motor vehicle
lubricants and spare parts will be done through public bidding.

We recommend that Management require the concerned personnel to comply with
the prescribed forms of the Purchase Request and Purchase Order, by adopting the
following corrective measures:
 the control on the preparation and numbering of PR and PO shall be lodged on
the GSO. The preparation and the numbering of the PR and PO should be
controlled by the GSO whose primary function is handling the supply and
management of the City’s supplies and equipment.
 the PR should have three signatories namely the Requisitioning Office, the City
Treasurer and approved by the City Mayor.
 the PO should only have two signatories, namely: the City Mayor and the
Supplier, acknowledging the receipt of the PO.

Management’s Comment(s):
Management committed to use the prescribed forms.

We recommend that Management strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016
Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 in the procurement of goods, particularly on the
prohibition to brand names to ensure a more competitive bidding and that prices
and quality of items that are availed of are the most advanatageous to the
government.

Management’s Comment(s):
Management committed not to include brand names in the Purchase Request.

We recommend that the Management require:
 the Accountant and the Supply Officer to maintain a complete and adequate
supplies ledger card and stock card, respectively, for every item on stock to
facilitate the recording and monitoring of purchases and issuances of supplies
from stock;
 the Accountant and the Supply Officer to conduct a periodic reconciliation of
balances between the two sets of records to determine any discrepancies and to
reconcile the periodic balance of the supply ledger card with the general ledger
balance;
 require the Supply Officer to prepare and consolidate weekly the RIS for
supplies and materials issued and report the same in the Summary of Supplies
and Materials Issued (SSMI); and
 require the Supply Officer to submit the SSMI together with the supporting
documents to the Accounting Section on time, as basis for recording in the
books.

We recommend that Management:
 stop the practice of splitting purchase requisitions or contracts;
 advice the BAC to minimize the practice of procuring goods and services by
“Shopping” and that bidding should be the primary mode of procurement in
compliance with R.A. 9184;
 advice the different department heads to prepare and submit a more reliable
individual PPMPs to the BAC for consolidation; and
 Direct the BAC, through the BAC Secretariat, to prepare the Annual
Procurement Plan (APP) to guide the City in the procurement of goods and
services.

We recommend that the City create the LCPC and provide allocation of 1% of the
IRA for the implementation of its PAPs in compliance with DILG MC No. 2012-120.
Formulate programs and/or activities utilizing the funds in the Trust Liability–DRRM to be presented to the LDRRMC and to be submitted to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for approval.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory taking
of all the City’s Property, Plant and Equipment.
b) Require the individual personnel who are directly accountable for lost or
damaged items during typhoon Yolanda to file for relief of property
accountability to facilitate the dropping of its value from the City’s books of
accounts.
c) Require the GSO-designate to dispose unserviceable properties amounting to
P10,060,701.02.
d) Fill-up the position for GSO, being a mandatory position for every Local
Government Units, as mandated in Section 490 of RA 7160 since this has long
been vacant even before the City of Bogo gained its charter to focus on the
control on the acquisition, maintenance, disposal and status of all properties of
the City.

We recommend that the City Mayor require the City General Services Office to
facilitate the preparation of documentary requirements to secure the Transfer
Certificate of Titles of purchased lots to support claims of ownership and to register
all real estate owned by the City of Bogo under the Torrens Title System pursuant to
COA Circular No. 92-386. Likewise, we recommend that the City Mayor create
an appraisal committee to conduct assessment of the value of donated lands to
facilitate recording in the books of accounts of the City notwithstanding the
availability of appropriated funds on this.

We recommend that the City Mayor require the City Planning Development Officer
to coordinate with the Local Development Council to identify and prioritize specific
Projects/Programs/Activities to immediately address the needs of its constituents
and to deliver timely public services through the use of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommend that Management calendar the implementation the projects. Require
the City Engineer to prepare the Program of Works, the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to undertake the procurement process and the City Planning Development
Council to monitor the implementation of the projects so that intended beneficiaries
could maximize the benefits derived therefrom.

We recommend that the City, through the City Planning and Development Officer,
identify programs/projects that will be funded out of the remaining loan proceeds
subject to the approval by the Sangguniang Panlungsod, to ensure that borrowed
funds are fully utilized and used for the benefits of the City’s constituents.

We recommend that the LDRRMO to formulate programs and/or activities utilizing
the funds in the Trust Liability–DRRM to be presented to the LDRRMC and to be
submitted to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for approval.

We recommend for the LDRRM Officer to advise the LDRRM Council to ensure
the acquisition of the needed equipment and vehicles, mindful of the
unpredictability of occurrence of either natural or man-made calamities.

We recommend that the City Planning Development Officer monitor projects and
likewise impose the liquidated damages to the contractors for failure on their part to
complete the projects within the specified contract time.

We recommend that City Officials and employees adhere strictly to the rules and
regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. We
recommend that the City issue demand letters to concerned employees for the
immediate liquidation of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and
Section 89 of PD 1445, otherwise, enforce the appropriate administrative and/or
legal sanctions.

We recommend that Management stop the practice of paying the payrolls of
employees to only one person whose name appears in the payroll but to require the
Disbursing Officer to withdraw a cash advance and liquidate it after payment.

We recommend that the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) initiates in
proposing to the City Mayor the formulation of the guidelines in granting wage rate
per day of Job Orders to at least the minimum wage as set by the National Wage
and Productivity Commission through the Regional Tripartite Wage and Productivity
Board VII, to be approved by the Sangguniang Panlungsod.
Moreover, he shall establish guidelines to be adopted in determining the wage rate
of every job order commensurate to their educational attainment, skills acquired
and other relevant factors.

We recommend that the City Accountant maintain sub-accounts consisting of
separate sets of ledgers for each of the enterprise and prepare separate trial
balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of
their respective operations, in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommend that the BAC adopt public bidding as a customary mode of
procurement and stop the practice of resorting to alternative modes of procurement
unless otherwise in exceptional cases. Likewise, we recommend that the City strictly
adhere to Section 53 of the Revised IRR of 9184.

Require all vehicles to be marked "For Official Use Only" together with the name and logo of the City.
Further, we recommend that ownership of vehicles bearing private plate numbers be transferred to the City.
Likewise, adopt measures to limit the use of government vehicles for official business only to prevent unnecessary expenses.
